title: Greetings
definition: Greetings are the first words we use when meeting someone. Choosing the right greeting depends on the time of day and how formal the situation is.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: Good morning! How are you today?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: Good morning! I'm doing well, thank you. How about you?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: Nice to see you. Have a great day!

practicePhrases: Good morning / Good afternoon / Good evening.

commonMistakes.why: 'Good night' is only used when leaving/going to sleep, not as a greeting when meeting someone in the evening.

commonMistakes.wrong: How do you do? I am fine.
commonMistakes.right: How do you do? Nice to meet you.
commonMistakes.why: 'How do you do?' is a formal greeting, not a real question — reply with the same phrase or 'Nice to meet you', not with 'fine'.

quiz.question: What do you say when meeting someone for the first time?
quiz.explanation: This is the standard phrase for a first meeting.

quiz.question: Which greeting is used in the evening when meeting someone?
quiz.explanation: 'Good evening' is used as a greeting; 'good night' is for leaving.

quiz.question: A: How ___ you? B: I'm fine, thank you.
quiz.explanation: 'How are you?' is the standard greeting question.
